name: GeoVax Labs, Inc.
summary: GeoVax Labs, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing human vaccines and immunotherapies against infectious diseases and solid tumor cancers. Its lead program, GEO-CM04S1, is a next-generation COVID-19 vaccine based on Modified Vaccinia Ankara (MVA) vector technology, currently in multiple Phase 2 trials and a BARDA-funded Phase 2b trial. Other programs include Gedeptin for head and neck cancer, GEO-MVA for Mpox/smallpox, and preclinical vaccines for Ebola, Marburg, and Zika.

products.name: GEO-CM04S1
products.description: Next-generation COVID-19 vaccine using sMVA vector expressing SARS-CoV-2 spike and nucleocapsid antigens, in multiple Phase 2 trials.

products.name: Gedeptin
products.description: Oncolytic solid tumor gene-directed therapy for advanced head and neck cancers; completed Phase 1/2, Phase 2 planned.

products.name: GEO-MVA
products.description: Vaccine targeting Mpox and smallpox; in regulatory discussions and manufacturing scale-up, clinical evaluation expected in 2025.

products.name: GEO-EM01-Z
products.description: Ebola Zaire vaccine candidate; completed non-human primate studies.

products.name: GEO-EM01-S
products.description: Ebola Sudan vaccine candidate; completed non-human primate studies.

products.name: GEO-MM01
products.description: Marburg vaccine candidate; completed non-human primate studies.

products.name: GEO-ZM02
products.description: Zika vaccine candidate; completed mouse model studies.

partnerships.description: BARDA-funded contract up to $45M to GeoVax for manufacturing and support of GEO-CM04S1 Phase 2b trial under Project NextGen.
CRO awarded $343M by BARDA to execute the 10,000-participant GEO-CM04S1 Phase 2b clinical trial as part of BARDA's Clinical Studies Network.
GeoVax licensed exclusive worldwide rights to develop and commercialize GEO-CM04S1 from COH in November 2021.
